description: 12-week rotating themes for 'AI in Your Business' section. Each week focuses on related tools and workflows.

themes.title: Your AI Command Center
themes.subtitle: Master the big 3 AI assistants before they master you

workflows.title: The 3-AI Stack for Any Business Question
workflows.description: Use Claude for complex analysis, ChatGPT for creative brainstorming, Perplexity for real-time research. Each excels at different things.
workflows.prompt: I'm building [your startup]. Help me think through [specific problem]. First, what questions should I be asking that I haven't considered?

workflows.title: Custom Instructions That Actually Work
workflows.description: Stop starting from scratch every conversation. Set up your AI to know your business context permanently.
workflows.prompt: You are my startup advisor. My company is [name], we [one-line pitch]. Our target customer is [description]. When I ask questions, consider: 1) My limited budget as a student founder 2) Speed to market over perfection 3) The Babson network as an asset

workflows.title: The Research-to-Action Pipeline
workflows.description: Turn any research session into clear next steps in under 10 minutes.
workflows.prompt: Based on this research, give me: 1) The single most important insight 2) Three specific actions I can take this week 3) One thing I should investigate further

themes.title: Validate Before You Build
themes.subtitle: Stop building things nobody wants. Here's how to know in 48 hours.

workflows.title: Competitor Analysis in 30 Minutes
workflows.description: Map your entire competitive landscape without spending weeks in spreadsheets.
workflows.prompt: I'm building [product description]. Find me: 1) The top 5 direct competitors with pricing 2) 3 indirect competitors I might be missing 3) Gaps in the market none of them serve 4) What customers complain about most in reviews

workflows.title: The 'Would They Actually Pay?' Test
workflows.description: Find out if your idea has legs before writing a single line of code.
workflows.prompt: Help me design a landing page test for [idea]. I need: 1) A headline that explains the value in <10 words 2) Three bullet points for the body 3) A CTA that measures real intent (not just interest) 4) What would make someone NOT sign up

workflows.title: Customer Interview Questions That Don't Suck
workflows.description: Stop asking leading questions. Get insights that actually change your product.
workflows.prompt: Generate 10 customer discovery questions for [target customer] about [problem]. Rules: No yes/no questions, no leading questions, focus on past behavior not future intent.

themes.title: Ship in a Weekend
themes.subtitle: You don't need to know how to code. These tools don't care.

workflows.title: Describe-to-Deploy in Plain English
workflows.description: Tell Cursor or Replit what you want. Watch it build. Ship before your next class.
workflows.prompt: Build me a simple web app that: [describe functionality]. Use: React, Tailwind CSS, and a simple backend. Make it work first, make it pretty second. Include basic error handling.

workflows.title: Landing Page in 15 Minutes
workflows.description: Your startup exists when it has a URL. Get one before lunch.
workflows.prompt: Create a landing page for [startup name]. We help [target customer] do [benefit] without [pain point]. Include: hero section, 3 benefit bullets, one testimonial placeholder, email signup, clean footer.

workflows.title: Debug Like a 10-Year Developer
workflows.description: Stop Googling error messages. Your AI pair programmer has seen this before.
workflows.prompt: I got this error: [paste error]. Here's my code: [paste relevant code]. What's wrong, why did it happen, and how do I fix it? Explain it like I'm a business student who just started coding.

themes.title: Look Legit Instantly
themes.subtitle: Your startup should look like a real company. 2 hours, zero design skills.

workflows.title: Brand Identity in One Session
workflows.description: Logo, colors, fonts, and brand voice. All consistent. All professional.
workflows.prompt: Create a brand guide for [startup name]. We're a [industry] company targeting [audience]. Our personality is [2-3 adjectives]. Give me: 1) 3 color palette options with hex codes 2) Font pairing recommendation 3) Logo concept descriptions 4) Brand voice guidelines (how we talk vs don't talk)

workflows.title: Social Media Templates That Match
workflows.description: Never wonder 'does this look right?' again. Create templates once, use forever.
workflows.prompt: Design a set of Instagram templates for [brand]. Include: 1) Quote post 2) Product/feature announcement 3) Customer testimonial 4) Behind-the-scenes 5) Educational carousel. Use colors [hex codes] and keep consistent spacing.

workflows.title: Product Mockups Without Photoshop
workflows.description: Show your product in context. Investors need to see the vision.
workflows.prompt: Create a realistic mockup showing [product/app] being used by [target user] in [context/setting]. Make it look professional and aspirational but believable. Style: modern, clean, natural lighting.

themes.subtitle: Win the room. Close the round.
themes.title: Pitch Perfect

workflows.title: Investor Deck in One Hour
workflows.description: The 10 slides every investor expects. AI writes them, you refine them.
workflows.prompt: Create a 10-slide pitch deck outline for [startup]. Include: Problem, Solution, Market Size, Business Model, Traction, Competition, Team, Financials, Ask, Vision. For each slide, write: the headline, 3 bullet points max, and what visual to include.

workflows.title: Practice Pitch with Brutal Feedback
workflows.description: Get ripped apart by AI before an investor does it in person.
workflows.prompt: I'm going to pitch you my startup. Act as a skeptical VC who has seen 1000 pitches. After I finish, give me: 1) The 3 biggest holes in my pitch 2) Questions you'd definitely ask 3) What almost made you interested 4) One thing to fix before my next pitch

workflows.title: Financial Model for Non-Finance People
workflows.description: Revenue projections that don't look ridiculous. Even if you failed accounting.
workflows.prompt: Help me build a simple 3-year financial model for [business]. Assumptions: [pricing, customer acquisition method, team size]. Give me: Monthly recurring revenue projection, key costs, break-even analysis, and the metrics investors will ask about.

themes.title: Fill Your Pipeline
themes.subtitle: Cold outreach that gets warm responses. No cringe required.

workflows.title: Cold Email That Gets Replies
workflows.description: The AI has read 10,000 cold emails. It knows what works.
workflows.prompt: Write a cold email to [target role] at [company type]. I want them to [desired action]. Keep it under 100 words. No fluff, no obvious templates. Reference something specific about their company. Make the ask crystal clear.

workflows.title: LinkedIn Outreach Without Being Annoying
workflows.description: Connect with purpose. Stop sounding like everyone else.
workflows.prompt: Write a LinkedIn connection request to [person type]. Context: [why I want to connect]. Keep it under 300 characters. Don't say 'I'd love to pick your brain' or 'I noticed we both...' Be specific and genuine.

workflows.title: Follow-Up Sequence That Closes
workflows.description: Most deals happen after the 5th touch. AI doesn't forget to follow up.
workflows.prompt: Create a 5-email follow-up sequence for [prospect type] who expressed interest but went quiet. Space them: Day 3, Day 7, Day 14, Day 21, Day 30. Each email should be shorter than the last. Final email should be a graceful close-the-loop.

themes.title: Create Like a Full Team
themes.subtitle: Blog posts, videos, podcasts. You're the whole content team now.

workflows.title: Blog Post From Idea to Published
workflows.description: Outline, draft, edit, optimize. 90 minutes total.
workflows.prompt: Write a blog post about [topic] for [audience]. It should: 1) Hook them in the first line 2) Include a surprising statistic or insight 3) Have 3 actionable takeaways 4) End with a clear CTA. Length: 800 words. Tone: conversational but credible.

workflows.title: Repurpose One Piece Into Five
workflows.description: Never start from scratch. Turn one idea into a content ecosystem.
workflows.prompt: I have this [blog post/video/podcast]. Turn it into: 1) A Twitter/X thread (10 tweets) 2) A LinkedIn post 3) An Instagram carousel script 4) A newsletter section 5) A YouTube Shorts script. Keep the core message but adapt the format.

workflows.title: Video Script That Keeps Attention
workflows.description: The first 3 seconds decide everything. AI knows the patterns.
workflows.prompt: Write a 60-second video script about [topic]. Structure: Hook (3 sec), Problem (10 sec), Solution (30 sec), Proof (10 sec), CTA (7 sec). Make the hook so good they can't scroll away.

themes.title: Own Your Feed
themes.subtitle: Stop posting randomly. Start building an audience that buys.

workflows.title: Content Calendar for the Month
workflows.description: Plan 30 days of posts in 30 minutes. Never stare at a blank screen again.
workflows.prompt: Create a 30-day social media calendar for [brand] on [platforms]. Mix: 40% value/educational, 30% behind-the-scenes/personal, 20% promotional, 10% engagement/questions. Include specific post ideas for each day.

workflows.title: Viral Hook Generator
workflows.description: The first line matters more than everything else combined.
workflows.prompt: Generate 20 hook options for a post about [topic]. Requirements: Pattern interrupt, specific (not vague), creates curiosity gap. Mix styles: contrarian takes, personal stories, data-driven, how-to.

workflows.title: Ad Copy That Converts
workflows.description: Stop boosting posts that don't work. Write ads that pay for themselves.
workflows.prompt: Write Facebook/Instagram ad copy for [product]. Target: [audience]. Goal: [conversion action]. Create: 3 headline options, 3 primary text options (short, medium, long), 3 CTA options. Use the PAS framework (Problem, Agitate, Solution).

themes.title: Run Like a Real Company
themes.subtitle: Systems that scale. Even when it's just you.

workflows.title: SOPs That Write Themselves
workflows.description: Document your processes once. Onboard anyone in minutes.
workflows.prompt: Create a standard operating procedure for [process]. Include: Overview (what and why), Step-by-step instructions, Common mistakes to avoid, Success metrics, FAQ. Write it so someone with zero context could follow it perfectly.

workflows.title: Meeting Notes to Action Items
workflows.description: Stop letting meetings disappear. Extract value from every call.
workflows.prompt: Here are my meeting notes: [paste notes]. Extract: 1) Key decisions made 2) Action items (who, what, when) 3) Open questions to resolve 4) Topics punted to next meeting. Format as a clean summary I can share with the team.

workflows.title: Weekly Review That Actually Works
workflows.description: Reflect on what matters. Plan what moves the needle.
workflows.prompt: Help me do my weekly review. Last week I: [what happened]. My goals were: [goals]. Questions to answer: What worked? What didn't? What should I do more of? What should I stop doing? What are my top 3 priorities for next week?

themes.title: Scale Your Support
themes.subtitle: Delight customers 24/7. Even while you're in class.

workflows.title: FAQ That Answers Before They Ask
workflows.description: Reduce support tickets by 70% with a knowledge base that actually helps.
workflows.prompt: Create a comprehensive FAQ for [product/service]. Include the 20 most common questions across: Getting started, Using the product, Billing, Troubleshooting, Account management. Write answers that are friendly but efficient.

workflows.title: Response Templates That Feel Personal
workflows.description: Fast replies that don't sound robotic. Your customers will think you're a bigger team.
workflows.prompt: Create 10 customer support response templates for [business]. Scenarios: New customer welcome, billing question, feature request, bug report, refund request, positive feedback, negative feedback, follow-up after resolution. Each should feel human and on-brand.

workflows.title: Turn Complaints Into Testimonials
workflows.description: The angry customer today is the raving fan tomorrow. Handle it right.
workflows.prompt: A customer just sent this complaint: [complaint]. Help me: 1) Acknowledge their frustration genuinely 2) Take ownership without over-apologizing 3) Offer a specific solution 4) Turn this into an opportunity to exceed expectations

themes.title: Decisions, Not Dashboards
themes.subtitle: Turn your spreadsheets into insights. No data science degree needed.

workflows.title: Spreadsheet Analysis in Plain English
workflows.description: Upload your data. Ask questions. Get answers.
workflows.prompt: Here's my data: [describe or paste]. Tell me: 1) What are the key trends? 2) What's surprising or unexpected? 3) What should I investigate further? 4) What decisions does this data support? 5) What data am I missing?

workflows.title: Metrics Dashboard for Your Startup
workflows.description: Track the 5 numbers that actually matter. Ignore everything else.
workflows.prompt: Help me set up a metrics dashboard for [business type]. Give me: 1) The 5 most important metrics to track 2) Why each matters 3) What 'good' looks like for a startup at my stage 4) How often to review each one 5) Warning signs to watch for

workflows.title: Survey Analysis Without the PhD
workflows.description: You ran the survey. Now understand what it means.
workflows.prompt: Analyze these survey responses: [paste responses]. Tell me: 1) Top 3 themes across all responses 2) Sentiment breakdown (positive/neutral/negative) 3) Specific quotes that capture key insights 4) Recommendations based on the feedback 5) Follow-up questions to ask

themes.title: Work Less, Do More
themes.subtitle: Build systems that run without you. The AI handles the busy work.

workflows.title: Identify What to Automate First
workflows.description: Not everything should be automated. Find the high-impact targets.
workflows.prompt: Here's how I spend my time each week: [list tasks and hours]. Identify: 1) Tasks that are repetitive and rule-based (automate these) 2) Tasks that need human judgment (keep these) 3) Tasks I should stop doing entirely 4) The one automation that would save me the most time

workflows.title: Build Your First Automation
workflows.description: No code required. Just describe what you want to happen.
workflows.prompt: I want to automate this: When [trigger happens], then [action should occur]. Walk me through setting this up in Zapier or Make. Include: What apps to connect, what fields to map, how to test it, common errors to avoid.

workflows.title: AI Agent That Works for You
workflows.description: Create a virtual employee that handles specific tasks automatically.
workflows.prompt: Design an AI agent for [specific task]. It should: 1) Monitor [input source] 2) Process using [criteria] 3) Take action by [output action] 4) Escalate to me when [conditions]. What tools would I connect? What prompts would I use?
